Mod 5 - Workbook Walkthrough (1) (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 3
Speaker 1: Hey guys, welcome to workbook for module. Glad you made it through the main video
lesson. Just to give you a bit of a recap, the launch sequence is the moment your
product first becomes available for sale. At this point you've shifted the beliefs, you've
built anticipation, and generate commitment throughout your segmentation,
confirmation, and prelaunch sequence, and now it's time to invite them to take that
simple next step into a reality that's fully aligned with that possibility you've engineered
together.
So even in the case of a webinar funnel, where you'll launch on the webinar itself, I still
encourage you to send out a dedicated launch email later in the day as well. This really
ensures that people who either didn't sign up, or signed up for the webinar but didn't
show up, get notified in a powerful way, and people who attended but didn't buy get a
prompt and powerful follow up that also reacquaints them with the reasons they should
enroll now and invest now.
What we're coaching here is going to be that progress and sustained momentum, that
Rome is still burning, and it's been burning, so extending that consistency principle into
the indefinite past, and as a well natural, but urgently necessary, next step. Think of that
wave metaphor, you've got to ride that momentum. It's too dangerous and too hard to
swim against that current. We're going to be coaching against procrastination. Coach
action by coaching against procrastination.
When you launch, and people assume that it's going to be around for three days, four
days, five days, seven days, procrastination is a very natural thing to occur, so we want
to coach against that. One way we explore doing that is that fast action bonus, which
will help you get that initial surge of sales, and act as that cart close light for non-action
takers, by getting them to feel that sting of missing out a few days before the final cart
close, you're in turn coaching them against inaction. You're showing them what it
actually feels like, like you are serious, you will pull this off.
Doing something like this, which is natural and legitimate, especially if you're offering a
fast action bonus that's not scalable and involves your time, it comes off as a lot more
sincere and accepted form of using FOMO, and urgency, and scarcity, than when you
just pound them with those messages later one, like five hours, four hours, three hours,
like one copy left of this digital version that's hosted on Teachable left. This is a way to
do it in a more authentic natural way that is real, doesn't just feel real but also coaches
people against procrastination. So even if they don't take you up right away, they at
least know that you're serious, know that there is something they could be missing out
on.
The questions I want us to explore, these are once again from the direct response 2.0 47
questions workbook, but you can explore them here again, and I suggest you do. But
what is the main magic one moment or outcome your product can deliver? Why is this
important to your prospect? Or why is it what they really need? On a level deeper, why
does that matter? If you can go deeper, why does that matter? Unravel that ball of
meaning, that yarn, and see where it goes.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Mod 5 - Workbook Walkthrough (1) (Completed 05/08/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 3
What new opportunities will arise as a result? How can we frame this outcome as a
push-pull that opens them up to new favorable possibilities? And what other intangible
or unexpected problems does buying your product solve? The big ones here, which we
explain in the 47 questions workbook, is freeing them from that search, and the
confidence to double down, to invest their time and effort and energy wisely, knowing
they're going to be getting that return.
Then we're going to also have those four levels of benefits they'll experience. The level
one, which is immediate and tangible. Then we have our immediate emotional, long
term tangible, and long term emotional. So you do want to arm yourself with a few of
those before we dive in. And then we actually have some questions specific to the
themeplates. The anti-launch launch themeplate, which you're soon going to see, and if
you have access to six figure emails, there's a full hour-long masterclass on this, so you
can go into that for a deeper dive. But if you're just rolling with what you have in front
of you today, here are the questions that's going to extract the content.
What micro-actions have they already taken that you can hold them consistent with?
How are they already in the process? That's what we want to answer there, show them
they're already moving and running out of that burning building. In what ways is Rome
burning for them? How has it been burning for long before you came along? You don't
want to give the perception that you started the fire. You want to give the perception
that you're leading them out of it.
How will your prospect be in a worse position if they buy in a few days? What will the
fire have already consumed between now and then? What benefits are they leaving the
table even if it's just three or four days later that they buy? How have they been trying
and struggling to put out the fire? Here's one that I'd love to explore. How is a product
launch disingenuous to you? What proprietary elements have been in development,
testing, being proven for months or years? What result or case studies can you point to
for that? That's going to be for the anti-launch launch themeplate.
For the Aladdin magic carpet ride themeplate, which is a similar one, and you'll see that
very, very soon, here are the additional questions. This is going to be for your minimum
viable funnel, where you're doing email only. So this email is going to work a little bit
harder, but you also want to know what the minimum viable commitment, and that's
something we're going to speak a lot more to in module seven. But essentially, what is
the minimum barrier to get in? Is it, test it for 30 days? Is it, I know you have your
doubts and hesitations but just give it a try, if it doesn't work no harm, no foul? What's
that minimum viable commitment look like for you?
What's the quickest and most valuable win your product can provide? Also another
major topic in module seven. We're bringing it in a little bit here because this is going to
be that minimum viable funnel, your product's first available, so you do want to include
some more of those elements.
For each of your features and benefits, I like to add what I call a magic carpet ride
moment. Which is that hyper specific positive and vivid real life result that accompanies
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

the benefit. I put together a little table for you here, so you can do this for your own
product. In the example of 10X launches, the feature would be 20 proven themeplatess.
So you can, that leads to the benefit, write high converting launch emails faster, and
which leads to the magic carpet ride moment. Fire it into ConvertKit while your copy's
still hot, and watch your SamCart notifications light up. That is crazy specific.
One sentence here we have ConvertKit, SamCart, Coffee, all these things that are super
familiar to them, and actually would resemble the process of achieving that benefit. So
feature, so you can benefit, and that should really lead you into being able to describe
what it looks like. You want to show them that world. You want to engage their
sensitivity. You want to be evocative in your language. That's what the magic carpet ride
moment does, and I want you to get in the habit of doing that for all your features and
benefits, because that's what adds that color to your copy. That's what makes your
future pacing feel real. That's what makes the results you're promising feel real.
No one wants to make money. No one really knows that means, but they know what it
means when their SamCart notifications light up, or they PayPal lights up. They know
what it's [inaudible 00:08:25] The thing you do before that is firing an email into
ConvertKit, or Drip, or ActiveCampaign, or Infusionsoft, those are things people are
familiar with, so that's why we use it. Be hyper specific in describing that magic carpet
ride moment.
That is it for the workbook. You do have a few exercises to get through before we go
into the themeplates, but once you do, once you're armed with the answers to these
questions, these magic carpet ride moments, the writing is going to be super simple and
fast later on. Give it a go, and I'll meet you back in themeplate eight, which is actually be
a little twist on this. It's the [inaudible 00:09:03] before and the never-too-early
ultimatum. I'll see you there in just a sec.
